title: Cone Volume Calculator (cm³ and Liters)
shortDescription: Calculate the volume of a cone from its radius and height.
description: Find the volume of a cone instantly. Enter the base radius and the height in centimeters, and get the result in cubic centimeters (cm³) and liters using the formula V = (1/3)·π·r²·h.
metaDescription: Free cone volume calculator. Enter radius and height in cm to get the volume in cm³ and liters using V = (1/3)·π·r²·h. Fast and accurate.

result.label: Volume
result.empty: Enter the radius and height to see the volume.

sections.heading: What is the volume of a cone?
paragraphs: A cone's volume is the amount of space it encloses. It equals exactly one third of a cylinder that shares the same base radius and height.
The formula is V = (1/3)·π·r²·h, where r is the base radius and h is the vertical height from base to apex.

sections.heading: Units and conversions
paragraphs: Enter the radius and height in centimeters and the result appears in cubic centimeters (cm³). Since 1 liter equals 1000 cm³, the calculator also shows the volume in liters.

howTo.name: Enter the radius
howTo.text: Type the base radius of the cone in centimeters.

howTo.name: Enter the height
howTo.text: Type the vertical height of the cone in centimeters.

howTo.name: Read the result
howTo.text: The volume is shown instantly in cm³ and liters.

faq.q: What is the formula for a cone's volume?
faq.a: V = (1/3)·π·r²·h, where r is the base radius and h is the height.

faq.q: What units does the calculator use?
faq.a: Radius and height are entered in centimeters, and the volume is shown in cm³ and liters.

faq.q: How do I convert cm³ to liters?
faq.a: Divide the value in cm³ by 1000, because 1 liter equals 1000 cm³.

faq.q: Is the height the slant height?
faq.a: No. Use the vertical height from the base to the apex, not the slant side.
No. Enter the radius, which is half of the base diameter.
